summary: Iter-17 backend sweep: Apple/Microsoft OAuth, S3 storage, WebSocket sync, Founder broadcast → 25/26 passed (1 confirmed spec-violation captured as xfail). All P0 endpoints from review_request work: GET /api/auth/oauth-providers returns correct booleans; POST /api/auth/apple + /api/auth/microsoft return 503 when unset, 401 with bogus token after configured; POST /api/founder/integrations/test/{apple,microsoft,s3} ok-toggles correctly on key set/clear; new founder integration fields (apple_client_id, microsoft_client_id, microsoft_tenant, aws_access_key_id, aws_secret_access_key, aws_region, aws_s3_bucket) all persist + appear masked in GET /founder/integrations with live_env_present:true; GET /api/storage/config returns ok:true, configured:false, 6 categories, user_id; POST /api/storage/presign-upload → 503 without AWS keys; GET /api/storage/list → ok:true, items:[]; WebSocket /api/ws/sync: valid JWT receives hello immediately; 2-socket fan-out works (socket A → socket B receives); POST /api/sync/sales triggers sync.updated event on connected WS; POST /api/founder/broadcast pushes founder.broadcast event to all connected sockets; GET /api/ws/stats returns users_connected + sockets_open ints; all regression endpoints (founder login, register/login/me, audit-log, subscription, fuel-prices/current, digest/preview, invites, ai/reconcile-mpesa) work.

minor.issue: SPEC violation: server echoes sync.write back to the SENDING socket. The review_request explicitly states 'should NOT echo back to self (server fans to OTHER sockets of same user)'. /app/backend/routers/ws.py:130 calls `await publish_to_user(user_id, msg)` which iterates ALL sockets in _connections[user_id] INCLUDING the sender. Fix: pass the sender ws as an exclude argument and skip it inside publish_to_user, e.g. `for ws in sockets: if ws is exclude: continue`. Captured as xfail in test_ws_hello_and_no_self_echo.

Without a valid token the server returns HTTP 403 on the WebSocket upgrade instead of accepting + closing with code 4401 as the spec states. Root cause in ws.py:104-107 — `websocket.close(code=4401)` is called BEFORE `websocket.accept()`, so Starlette short-circuits with a 403 rejection and the custom 4401 code never reaches the client. Fix: either `await websocket.accept()` then `await websocket.close(code=4401)`, or document the 403 as the intended unauth behavior. Tests accept both kinds today.

action_items: [P1] In /app/backend/routers/ws.py, change publish_to_user() to accept an optional `exclude: WebSocket` arg and skip it in the fan-out loop; in the ws_sync handler pass `exclude=websocket` when relaying client-originated messages so the sender doesn't receive its own echo. Spec violation reported by review_request.

[P2] In /app/backend/routers/ws.py:104-107, accept the WebSocket first (`await websocket.accept()`) then close with code 4401 so the close frame actually carries the documented 4401 code instead of being short-circuited to an HTTP 403 upgrade rejection. Browsers don't see 4401 today.

[P3] (Carry-over from iter-16) DELETE /api/founder/integrations/{field} pops os.environ but does not reload the .env baseline — clearing a runtime-pasted STRIPE_API_KEY still leaves stripe broken until backend restart. Suggest load_dotenv(override=False) after pop, or keep a startup snapshot of os.environ for restore.
critical_code_review_comments: ws.py is otherwise solid — lock-guarded set per user, heartbeat coroutine, prune-on-failure, broadcast_all helper. Just needs the sender-exclude filter and the accept-before-close ordering fix.

storage.py correctly namespaces by user id (users/{uid}/{cat}/...) and uses presigned URLs (avoids streaming through FastAPI) — good design. Auth check on download/delete correctly prevents cross-tenant access via key prefix.

oauth_extra.py JWKS cache with TTL + rotation refresh on miss is correct. Apple/Microsoft 503-when-unconfigured is the right UX (rather than 500). _upsert_oauth_user idempotently merges auth_methods which is good for users who originally registered via password.

founder_ops.py _KEY_TO_ENV map cleanly includes all 19 new fields (apple/microsoft/aws). Audit-log entries on every mutation. Masked-only response on GET /founder/integrations protects the founder UI from leaking full secrets.

success_rate.backend: 100% on must-pass (25/25) + 1 xfail capturing the spec violation. Sender-self-echo bug confirmed.

test_credentials: FOUNDER / publican1D#20 (confirmed). Fresh TEST_iter17_*@fuelpro.app users created via /api/auth/register (password=password123).
seed_data_creation: 2 TEST_iter17_*@fuelpro.app users in db.users; ~1 sync_sales row; a few audit_log entries (founder.broadcast, founder.integrations_updated, user.register, storage.presign_upload skipped); runtime_config integration keys are SET then DELETED at the end of each test for cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run /app/backend/tests/test_iter17_features.py for the iter-17 regression set (covers OAuth providers, S3 storage, WebSocket sync, founder broadcast, ws stats, and a small backend regression core). For WebSocket tests use the `websockets` Python library; convert https→wss and remember tokens go as ?token= query string, not as a header. The xfail test_ws_hello_and_no_self_echo will flip to XPASS once the main agent fixes ws.py to exclude the sender — update the @pytest.mark.xfail(strict=False) line to remove the marker once it does. Cleanup at end of class TestFounderIntegrationsCRUD deletes all aws_*/apple/microsoft keys; no manual cleanup required.
rca of the issue: Two WebSocket spec deviations confirmed in /app/backend/routers/ws.py: (1) `publish_to_user(user_id, msg)` at line 130 fans to every socket in the user's connection set, including the sender — review_request explicitly forbids echo-to-self; (2) line 104-107 calls `await websocket.close(code=4401)` BEFORE `await websocket.accept()`, which Starlette translates into an HTTP 403 upgrade-reject and DROPS the custom close code. Both are small, localized fixes: add `exclude: Optional[WebSocket]=None` to publish_to_user and `if ws is exclude: continue` inside the fan-out loop, and reorder accept-then-close-with-code in the unauth branch. All other iter-17 functionality (oauth_extra, storage, founder integration keys, sync.updated + founder.broadcast WS events, ws_stats) works correctly per spec.
